20 views 2mo ago

Image 15: Wils

Wils

•6 reviews

Love IKI and what they do!!! It is definitely one of the few decent LLM + personal knowledge base systems that I have come across. Unlimited uploads of your docs, transcribes YouTube vids, and you can also save webpages. From what I remember, they utilise knowledge graph algorithms to organise all the docs you upload, which means you can drill down or get a broader understanding pretty quickly. (Pretty fond of the good ole knowledge graph). Also, found that the answers are less "bias" when you need a critical approach, which I've struggled with in the past. Initially, I jumped on board because of their paralegal version (which I never accessed), but am now kinda just in love with the product in general.

#### What's great

AI Chatbots [...] ### Best for

Best for terminal-first engineers who want an autonomous agent to implement, run, and iterate on real codebase tasks.

### Standout features

   ✓Terminal-native agentic workflow execution
   ✓Plan mode for scoped task outlines
   ✓Skills system for repeatable processes
   ✓Parallel sub-agents for faster progress
   ✓Memory for repo preferences across sessions

## Windsurf

Image 20: WindsurfWindsurf
The first agentic IDE. Tomorrow's editor, today.

4.7 ·65 reviews

Learn more →

Windsurf takes the familiar IDE experience and pushes it toward full agentic execution with Cascade. Compared to Cursor’s prompt-and-apply loop, Windsurf aims to do more automatically: it can create files, run commands, iterate on failures, and keep pushing until it reaches a working result.

AI Code Editors [...] One of its biggest differentiators is flexibility in model choice. Cline is designed for quickly switching between LLM providers and configurations, which is valuable if cost, latency, or capability varies by task and you don’t want to commit to one model ecosystem.

It’s also a practical pick for fast prototyping, especially for UI experimentation where rapid iterations matter more than heavyweight process. The product is geared toward helping you move from idea to working code in the editor, then refine from there.

If the priority is VS Code-native autonomy plus freedom to experiment with different models, Cline can be a better fit than stacks optimized around a single agent runtime and a more fixed workflow shape.

### Best for [...] ### Best for

Ideal for VS Code-centric developers who want an autonomous agent and multi-model flexibility.

### Standout features

 ✓VS Code-native autonomous agent
 ✓Broad LLM provider and model selection
 ✓Rapid prototyping and iteration
 ✓Agent-driven end-to-end changes
 ✓Extensible workflow via MCP tooling

## LogiCoal

LogiCoalLogiCoal

AI multi-agent coding assistant for your terminal

Learn more →

Logicoal focuses on a terminal-based, orchestrator-driven way to run multiple agents without losing the thread. Instead of treating each agent as a separate chat, it’s built around a main orchestrator that retains full session context and delegates specialized slices of work to subagents.

results.title: Omnifact: Privacy First AI Chat Assistants for Businesses | Product Hunt
results.content:
for Various AI Models: We’re provider-independent and treat self-hosted models as first-class citizens. 4. Exceptional Usability: Anyone in your organization can create AI assistants, not just tech experts. 🤔 Why Omnifact? As open-source LLMs continue to advance, self-hosted models are enabling new use cases across industries that can’t rely on cloud-hosted LLM providers due to regulatory constraints. Omnifact empowers these companies with a platform that fully supports and optimizes self-hosted models while making AI accessible throughout the organization. We believe AI adoption will significantly impact business efficiency, but challenges like integration, privacy, and security have slowed progress. Omnifact addresses these hurdles head-on, enabling businesses to iterate towards [...] Hi @irida Thanks for the question! We take data privacy and security very seriously at Omnifact. One of the key ways we ensure this is through our advanced data privacy filter. Think of it as a safety net for your sensitive information. Here’s how it works: Before any data is sent to an LLM (even for processing), our filter automatically scans and identifies potentially sensitive information. This could include names, addresses, financial details, and more. - You have complete control. You can customize the filter to align with your industry's specific privacy regulations and your organization's policies. - Sensitive data gets masked. The identified information is then replaced with placeholders or anonymized, ensuring that the original data remains protected. - This means you can [...] Report
